operating_state: Total net sales increased 9% to $97.3 billion driven by Services growth of 17%, iPhone growth of 5%, and Mac growth of 15%, while iPad declined 2%. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in Americas and Europe, flat performance in Japan, and decline in Rest of Asia Pacific. Services growth reflected higher advertising, App Store and cloud services revenue, while iPhone benefited from new model launches and Mac from MacBook Pro strength.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 43.7% from 42.5% driven by improved product mix and leverage. Products gross margin increased to 36.4% while Services gross margin expanded to 72.6% from different mix and improved leverage, partially offset by foreign currency headwinds. Operating expenses increased as R&D grew from headcount and engineering program costs while SG&A rose from advertising and headcount increases.
material_events: Apple launched iPhone SE with 5G, iPad Air with M1 chip, Mac Studio with M1 Max/Ultra chips, and Apple Studio Display during the quarter. The company repurchased $22.9 billion of common stock and paid $3.6 billion in dividends. The Board raised quarterly dividend from $0.22 to $0.23 per share and increased share repurchase authorization by $90 billion.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues affecting operations through retail store capacity limitations and supply chain disruptions from outsourcing partners and component suppliers. Foreign currency movements created net unfavorable impacts in Europe and Rest of Asia Pacific while Japanese yen weakness offset underlying growth and Chinese renminbi strength provided favorable impact. New U.S. Treasury regulations negatively impacted foreign tax credits, raising effective tax rate.
forward_outlook: Management expects gross margins to remain subject to volatility and downward pressure while continuing focused R&D investments for future growth. Apple intends to increase dividends annually subject to Board approval and maintains manufacturing purchase obligations primarily payable within 12 months. The company believes current cash balances plus operational cash flow will satisfy requirements over the next 12 months and beyond.
uncertainty_profile: Supply chain disruptions from COVID-19 variants and protective measures could continue affecting worldwide sales through outsourcing partner and component supplier impacts, with pandemic effects remaining uncertain. Manufacturing purchase obligations are primarily noncancelable, creating committed cash outflows. Foreign currency volatility continues impacting international operations across major regions.
